summary: The student asked for the types of events considered disasters in a Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP) context. The assistant explained that a disaster is any event disrupting normal operations and listed several categories: natural disasters (earthquakes, floods, etc.), pandemics/epidemics, cyber attacks (malware, DDoS, ransomware), other intentional human-caused threats (terrorism, biochemical attacks), technological hazards (power outages, accidents), and machine/hardware failure.
